notes: Instantiation (b) of architect-editor-pair for codex: a plan->implement MODE pair (one model) captured across the approval gate, the post-approval CONTINUATION of codex 2-18 (user-blocking-plan-mode-approval) which terminates at the waiting gate. Asserts the FULL arc ready->working->waiting->working->ready on ONE live codex rollout session. The architect/plan phase is codex Plan Mode (entered via the /plan slash, TUI-only); the agent emits a <proposed_plan> block which the codex parser synthesizes into a SYNTHETIC ExitPlanMode tool_use whose open call survives task_complete, so the turn ends in waiting (the plan-review gate) — no intervening ready. The user APPROVES with a follow-up message that clears the open tool (ClearToolNames) and directs codex to apply a REAL file edit (apply_patch creating calc.py) + run `ls`; that multi-step recovery turn makes the waiting->working->ready episode observable (a trivial text-only reply would coalesce into a direct waiting->ready inside the daemon's 2s debounce window). Two prose-asserted signals verified at record time (the phase DSL has no token/model field): SessionMetrics.TotalTokens after turn_end > at plan_gate (tokens accumulate across the plan and implement phases), and ModelName is a single consistent value across both phases (one model — the mode-pair instantiation, not the Aider two-model shape). Every phase is same_session_as the rollout session_birth so the post-approval recovery + real edit are proven on the SAME session that gated.

text: Pre-session (proc-<PID>) appears in ready within 1s of codex launch — the process-presence row, distinct from the rollout session

Rollout session reconciles with a DISTINCT session_id (different from the presession), born ready. The rollout file materializes lazily — only after /plan enters Plan Mode and the first plan-request turn begins writing — so it can appear tens of seconds after the presession; the window covers that codex-TUI-boot + Plan-Mode-entry + lazy-rollout latency (run-to-run jitter), not a behavioral claim.
User sends the plan-request prompt in Plan Mode -> ready->working (architect/plan phase begins on the rollout session)

Agent emits a <proposed_plan> block -> the codex parser synthesizes a SYNTHETIC ExitPlanMode that keeps a tool call open across task_complete -> working->waiting (the plan-review gate). The 'no ready before approval' invariant is enforced STRUCTURALLY by the phase chain: plan_gate is the first waiting after architect_plan(working), editor_implement is the first working after plan_gate, and turn_end is the first ready after editor_implement — so the ordering working->waiting->working->ready itself proves no ready interposed between plan emission and the approval recovery. Unlike 2-18, this cell DOES go ready after approval.
The waiting episode is visible and persists while the agent is gated on the user's plan decision — no skip-over to ready before the user approves

User approves the plan and asks the agent to implement -> waiting->working (editor/implement phase begins; the user message clears the open ExitPlanMode). The recovery turn applies a real apply_patch edit creating calc.py and runs `ls`, so this working span survives the daemon's 2s debounce window and is observable, not coalesced into a direct waiting->ready
Implement turn finishes (real apply_patch applied; calc.py created; `ls` run; summary given) -> working->ready
